issues.title: Deep Pavement Crater outside Westside High School
issues.description: Massive pothole has opened up in the middle of Broad Street right next to the school crossing. It forces cars to swerve into oncoming traffic, posing a major hazard during school hours.
issues.resolvedNotes: The Roads & Transportation repair crew deployed an asphalt patch truck within 24 hours of reporting. The pothole has been fully excavation-filled and re-paved. School crossing markings have been restored.

gps.address: 452 Broad St, New York, NY 10007 (Adjacent to Westside High)

verifications.comment: Confirmed! Saw school buses struggling to dodge this yesterday morning. Awesome to see it patched up so fast!

impact.environmentalImpact: Negligible. Minor dust dispersion from loose road substrate.
impact.explanation: High severity due to the extreme danger to student safety during school drop-off and pickup. Swerving vehicles increase head-on collision risks near a pedestrian-dense school zone.

issues.priorityReason: High risk due to proximity of high school and rapid community support.

resolution.suggestedResolution: Excavate damaged base layers, apply hot-mix asphalt patching, compact with drum roller, and paint school crossing markings.

issues.title: Burst Water Main Flooding Emergency Entrance
issues.description: A high-pressure public supply line has ruptured, causing thousands of gallons of water to flood the street, completely blocking the Mercy Hospital emergency ambulance lane.

gps.address: 78 Mercy Hospital Blvd, New York, NY 10013

verifications.comment: Ambulances are currently being diverted to the side gate. Extremely critical!

impact.environmentalImpact: Potable water wasting, local street erosion, and basement flooding.
impact.explanation: Extremely critical risk because it obstructs ambulances from entering the city's main Emergency Response facility. Any delay in emergency services directly threatens human lives.

issues.priorityReason: Critical score because hospital access is compromised and hospital operations are heavily affected.

resolution.suggestedResolution: Shut off local control valves, excavate surrounding street, replace the burst 12-inch cast-iron pipeline segment, backfill, and restore emergency paving.

issues.title: Industrial Waste and Scrap Metal Dump
issues.description: Massive pile of rusted metal scrap, discarded car batteries, and hazardous waste dumped overnight directly on Central Park's jogging trail. Children often play in this green area.
issues.resolvedNotes: The Waste Management hazardous response team collected all toxic materials, including 4 leaking car batteries. Scrap metals were loaded into haulers and sent to recycling centers. The soil has been treated and verified toxic-free.

gps.address: Central Park East Jogging Trail (Corner of 5th Ave & 72nd St)

impact.environmentalImpact: Acid leak from batteries into local park soil, threatening nearby water table.
impact.explanation: The inclusion of lithium-acid batteries makes this an environmental hazard. Rusted scrap metal also poses tetanus risks to children playing nearby.

issues.priorityReason: High score due to high toxic threat to park ecosystem and massive community verified responses.

resolution.suggestedResolution: Dispatch hazardous waste team with specialized transport, clear battery acid footprint, soil remediation, and recycle steel scrap.

issues.title: Damaged Storm Pole & Hanging Live Wires
issues.description: Strong winds cracked a wooden utility pole, leaving it leaning at a 45-degree angle. High voltage wires are hanging down just 6 feet above the busy pedestrian sidewalk.

impact.environmentalImpact: Potential electric fires and power grids disruption.
impact.explanation: Critical danger of fatal electrocution to any pedestrians walking on Grand Street. The leaning pole could completely collapse, ripping down main lines and causing a widespread local blackout.

issues.priorityReason: Extreme danger of pedestrian contact with active 13kV power lines.

resolution.suggestedResolution: De-energize the grid block, install temporary wire support, replace utility pole with concrete alternative, re-tension power wires, and safety-verify voltage ground.
